title: Google caps Meta's Gemini use as AI demand strains capacity
summary: Google has capped Meta's access to its Gemini AI models as exploding demand for advanced AI systems makes compute capacity the tech industry's scarcest strategic resource. The constraint signals that even top-tier companies face access limits to frontier AI infrastructure, creating a new competitive dynamic in enterprise AI adoption. Computing power shortages are poised to shape which companies can scale AI products and which remain bottlenecked.

title: Bond giant Pimco flexes muscles with private placements push
summary: Pimco is expanding aggressively into private placements, targeting borrowers who need to raise capital outside traditional public markets as the lines between public and private debt continue to blur. The world's largest bond manager is leveraging its scale to compete for mandates in a segment traditionally dominated by banks and smaller credit funds. Pimco's push reflects how institutional asset managers are competing for private credit deal flow as the alternative credit market matures.

title: Chinese tech hub's shift into robotaxis leaves drivers by the wayside
summary: Shenzhen is rapidly scaling its driverless robotaxi fleet as China's AI-in-mobility push accelerates, but the expansion is displacing gig economy workers who are losing livelihoods to automation. The city's experience provides a real-world case study of how AI deployment in transportation disrupts employment even as it creates new mobility options. The Shenzhen model is being watched globally as a benchmark for how cities will manage AI-driven economic displacement.

title: AI Fever Powers HK Share Sales Through Hurdles to Five-Year High
summary: Investor enthusiasm around the AI boom drove Hong Kong share sales to a five-year high in the first half of 2026, overcoming headwinds from a sluggish equity market and regulatory challenges. The surge reflects how AI-driven investor sentiment is generating capital markets activity across Asian markets despite local structural weaknesses. The trend suggests that AI optimism is powerful enough to sustain IPO and equity capital activity even in unfavorable market conditions.

title: La IA y el nearshoring elevarán 10% la demanda eléctrica, pero el capital llega lento
summary: La inteligencia artificial y el nearshoring impulsarán un incremento de hasta 10% en la demanda eléctrica de México, creando una presión significativa sobre la infraestructura energética del país. Sin embargo, el financiamiento para la transición energética avanza lentamente, lo que podría convertirse en un cuello de botella que limite el potencial de México como destino de manufactura e inversión tecnológica. La brecha entre la demanda proyectada y la capacidad de inversión representa un riesgo estructural para el crecimiento industrial que el nearshoring está generando.

title: Tech Equity Sales Renew AI Debt-Binge Worries
summary: Technology companies are selling equity at a pace reminiscent of the dot-com boom, prompting bond investors to worry that AI-driven capital requirements are loading balance sheets with unsustainable debt. The surge in tech equity issuance reflects the massive funding needs of AI infrastructure buildout but raises questions about corporate credit quality at scale. Market observers are increasingly concerned that the AI spending cycle could create overleveraged balance sheets similar to those seen in previous tech booms.

title: AI's Energy Crunch Has Investors Searching for Next IPO Winners
summary: The AI boom's insatiable demand for power has created a new investment frontier, with Wall Street betting billions on energy companies that promise to solve the compute-power bottleneck through IPOs and private capital raises. Investors are committing capital even to companies whose technology hasn't been fully developed, reflecting the urgency of the AI infrastructure buildout. The AI energy crunch is generating a new class of capital markets winners in power generation, grid infrastructure, and data center cooling.

title: Tech Slump Deepens
summary: Technology stocks fell sharply as investors questioned the sustainability of the AI investment thesis, with rising semiconductor and memory costs compressing margins and US restrictions on advanced AI model exports adding a regulatory overhang. Analysts debated whether AI companies can generate sufficient revenue to justify sky-high valuations as capital spending requirements continue to escalate. The week's correction is forcing a reckoning about which players in the AI ecosystem have truly defensible business models.

title: Building Public Life: How Bogotá and Mexico City Addressed Urban Inequality
summary: ArchDaily examines community infrastructure projects in Bogotá and Mexico City — including Mexico City's PILARES civic centers — that use architecture to address the absence of public gathering, learning, and cultural spaces in historically underserved peripheral neighborhoods. Rather than focusing only on housing or transit, these projects invest in multi-functional civic buildings that merge education, culture, and recreation. The article positions this approach as a model for how Latin American cities can build social infrastructure alongside physical infrastructure.

title: Así son las Meta Glasses by Kylie Jenner: precio y funciones de los lentes con IA
summary: Meta lanzó las Meta Glasses by Kylie Jenner, una colaboración que integra inteligencia artificial en gafas de uso cotidiano disponibles desde $399 dólares, con cámara de 12 MP y la voz de Jenner como asistente AI personalizado, en alianza con EssilorLuxottica. El lanzamiento forma parte de la estrategia de Meta para masificar su plataforma de hardware con IA a través de alianzas con íconos culturales, compitiendo directamente en el segmento de wearables inteligentes. El producto posiciona a Meta como contendiente serio en el mercado de gafas con IA frente a competidores como Google y Apple.

title: Anthropic's Mythos 5 AI Model Cleared by US for Wider Use
summary: Anthropic received US government approval to restore access to its Mythos 5 AI model after resolving the Trump administration's national security concerns about the technology's potential risks. The clearance allows trusted partners to use the model, marking a significant regulatory milestone following weeks of negotiation between the company and federal authorities. The development illustrates the evolving framework Washington is building to govern frontier AI deployment while preserving domestic AI competitiveness.

title: Forum AI CEO on Pitfalls With AI in Politics
summary: Forum AI co-founder and CEO Campbell Brown called for AI companies to open themselves to greater external scrutiny as the technology plays an expanding role in political processes. The discussion highlighted specific risks of AI in political contexts — including manipulation, accountability gaps, and AI-generated content distorting democratic discourse — with no adequate regulatory framework yet in place. The call comes as AI's role in the 2026 US midterm elections is growing rapidly across campaign data operations, advertising, and content.

title: Anthropic Moves Toward Deal With US to Lift Curbs on AI Models
summary: Anthropic and the Trump administration are converging on an agreement to lift US restrictions on the company's top two AI models after weeks of security-focused negotiations over the systems' potential national security implications. The deal would restore commercial and research access to Anthropic's most capable systems, which had been restricted pending a security review. The agreement reflects how Washington is managing a dual mandate: preserving US AI leadership while applying selective guardrails to frontier models.

title: AI Trade's Bruising Week Forces Investors to Be More Selective
summary: A volatile week in AI-related stocks is prompting institutional investors to apply greater selectivity in backing AI companies, distinguishing between those with proven fintech and enterprise revenue traction versus speculative pure-play bets. Domain Money's founder discussed how early-stage AI startup mechanics and VC dynamics are evolving as investor scrutiny of business models intensifies. The market correction signals a maturing of AI investment from a rising-tide moment to one requiring more rigorous due diligence.

title: Nasdaq y S&P 500 suman cinco días de pérdidas, mientras se acelera la rotación fuera de la tecnología
summary: El Nasdaq y el S&P 500 acumularon cinco días consecutivos de pérdidas al cierre de semana, con las ventas concentradas en fabricantes de semiconductores que habían registrado ganancias históricas durante el trimestre, en medio de un sentimiento pesimista creciente sobre el comercio de IA. Sin embargo, el índice ponderado por igual del S&P 500 tocó un máximo histórico, indicando una rotación sectorial más que una caída sistémica del mercado. La divergencia entre grandes tecnológicas y el resto del mercado sugiere una redistribución del capital hacia sectores distintos, con implicaciones para estrategias de asignación de portafolios.

title: Villa Riviera Golf Residence / Atelier130
summary: Atelier130's Villa Riviera Golf Residence is a 600-square-meter luxury home in Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire, designed to integrate harmoniously within a golf course setting, with photography by Alejandra Loreto. The project explores the relationship between the open landscape of a golf course and the programmatic requirements of a private high-end residence, creating a dialogue between sport and domestic architecture. It exemplifies the premium market for golf-adjacent residential development in emerging luxury real estate markets.

title: OpenAI Limits Release of New Model Under Pressure From US
summary: OpenAI is staging the rollout of a more capable AI model — initially available to select partners only — following direct pressure from the Trump administration to stagger broad public access releases. The move mirrors similar restrictions applied to Anthropic's models, signaling a government-wide approach to controlling the pace of frontier AI deployment for national security reasons. The staged release pattern suggests Washington is establishing a de facto approval process for next-generation AI capabilities across leading labs.

title: AI Is Already Reshaping US Politics at Every Level
summary: From data center political backlash to tech billionaire campaign spending and fears about deepfaked campaign ads, AI is permeating every level of US electoral politics ahead of the 2026 midterms. Silicon Valley money is funding AI-driven political operations while regulators struggle to establish guardrails for AI-generated campaign content. The feature examines how AI is emerging simultaneously as a tool of political power and a contested governance frontier, with implications for AI regulation globally.

title: Island Crest / ONOMA Architecture
summary: ONOMA Architecture's Island Crest is a 4,000-square-foot residential project on Mercer Island in Washington state, photographed by Rafael Soldi. The design showcases contemporary Pacific Northwest residential architecture in a premium island setting that balances natural landscape with sophisticated interiors. The project represents the caliber of high-end single-family architecture emerging in the greater Seattle area's luxury residential market.
